perturbation.original_text: In the event that the Parties or any of their Representatives are requested pursuant to, or required by, applicable law, regulation, rules of any national stock exchange, or legal process to disclose any of the Information, the Parties must notify each other promptly so that they may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy or, in the sole discretion of the party that initially furnished such Information, waive compliance with the terms of this letter agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event that the Parties or any of their Representatives are requested pursuant to, or required by, applicable law, regulation, rules of any national stock exchange, or legal process to disclose any of the Disclosed Material, the Parties must notify each other promptly so that they may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy or, in the sole discretion of the party that initially furnished such Information, waive compliance with the terms of this letter agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Using "Disclosed Material" in place of "Information" in the first part of the sentence creates an ambiguity, where the second part of the same sentence continues to refer back to the originally defined term "Information".
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event that the Parties or any of their Representatives are requested pursuant to, or required by, applicable law, regulation, rules of any national stock exchange, or legal process to disclose any of the Disclosed Material, the Parties must notify each other promptly so that they may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy or, in the sole discretion of the party that initially furnished such Information, waive compliance with the terms of this letter agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Information' with 'Disclosed Material' in the first part of the sentence, while the latter part of the same sentence refers to 'Information'. The contradiction explanation states that this creates ambiguity as the sentence initially refers to 'Disclosed Material' but then switches back to the originally defined term 'Information', creating an in-text contradiction according to the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition. The contradicted text section is the same sentence, further highlighting the direct conflict created by the change.

perturbation.original_text: If COMPANY or COMSYS determines not to proceed with the Transaction, they will promptly inform the other Party of that decision and, in that case, and at any time upon request of COMPANY or COMSYS or any of their Representatives, the other party will either (i) promptly destroy all copies of the written Information in its or its Representatives’ possession and confirm such destruction to the initiating company in writing or (ii) promptly deliver to the initiator of such action all copies of the written Information in its or its Representatives’ possession.
perturbation.changed_text: If COMPANY or COMSYS determines not to proceed with the Transaction, they will promptly inform the other Party of that decision and, in that case, and at any time upon request of COMPANY or COMSYS or any of their Representatives, the other party will either (i) promptly destroy all copies of the written Confidential Details in its or its Representatives’ possession and confirm such destruction to the initiating company in writing or (ii) promptly deliver to the initiator of such action all copies of the written Information in its or its Representatives’ possession.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Information" to "Confidential Details" (undefined) in option (i) but retaining 'Information' in option (ii) creates a contradiction. It's unclear which information must be destroyed and which must be returned.
perturbation.contradicted_text: If COMPANY or COMSYS determines not to proceed with the Transaction, they will promptly inform the other Party of that decision and, in that case, and at any time upon request of COMPANY or COMSYS or any of their Representatives, the other party will either (i) promptly destroy all copies of the written Confidential Details in its or its Representatives’ possession and confirm such destruction to the initiating company in writing or (ii) promptly deliver to the initiator of such action all copies of the written Information in its or its Representatives’ possession. Any oral Information will continue to be subject to the terms of this letter agreement. Notwithstanding anything contained in the foregoing to the contrary, either Party may retain in the files of its legal counsel such documents and records as are required to be maintained in order to satisfy any law, rule, or regulation to which the Party is subject or for the purpose of determining its continuing obligations under this Agreement, including any rule of any national stock exchange.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'Information' with 'Confidential Details' in option (i) but not in option (ii) of the same clause. This creates an in-text contradiction because the meaning of what should be destroyed versus what should be returned becomes ambiguous. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text in this case. This constitutes misaligned terminology as per the definitions provided, creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Parties hereby agree that money damages may not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement. Accordingly, in the event of any breach of the provisions of this Agreement, the breaching party agrees that the non-breaching party may be entitled to equitable relief, including injunction and specific performance.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties hereby agree that money damages may not be a sufficient compensation for any breach of this Agreement. Accordingly, in the event of any breach of the provisions of this Agreement, the breaching party agrees that the non-breaching party may be entitled to equitable relief, including injunction and specific performance.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "remedy" to "compensation" in the first sentence of Section 6, but continuing to refer to 'remedy' in the same section creates an in-text contradiction. While both refer to a solution to a problem, it is unclear what the change implies
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties hereby agree that money damages may not be a sufficient compensation for any breach of this Agreement. Accordingly, in the event of any breach of the provisions of this Agreement, the breaching party agrees that the non-breaching party may be entitled to equitable relief, including injunction and specific performance. Such remedy may not be deemed to be the exclusive remedy for the breach of this Agreement but may be in addition to all other remedies available at law or equity to the non-breaching party. In the event that the non-breaching party successfully enforces the obligations of the breaching party hereunder, the breaching party shall reimburse the non-breaching party for all reasonable costs and expenses, including legal counsel, incurred by the non-breaching party in this regard.
perturbation.justification: The explanation states that changing 'remedy' to 'compensation' in the Changed Text, while maintaining the word 'remedy' later in the same section (as is shown in the Contradicted Text), creates an in-text contradiction under the 'Misaligned Terminology' perturbation type. The original text uses 'remedy' consistently. The change introduces ambiguity about whether 'remedy' and 'compensation' are interchangeable, leading to potential confusion regarding the parties' rights and obligations.
